title: Capsella belgica
taxonomy: Capsella belgica is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. It belongs to the genus Capsella, which consists of annual or biennial plants. The genus Capsella is part of the tribe Brassiceae within the Brassicaceae family. Capsella belgica is native to Europe and Western Asia, where it grows in a variety of habitats. It is closely related to other species within the genus Capsella, such as Capsella bursa-pastoris, also known as shepherd's purse.

Capsella bursa-pastoris, commonly known as Shepherds Purse, is a small annual plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including menstrual cramps, diarrhea, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The bioactive constituents of Shepherds Purse include glucosinolates, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Shepherds Purse include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried and fresh leaves used in salads and as a garnish.
